description:
ICICI Lombard has introduced  MaxProtect, the latest addition to its range of customer-focused products. 

MaxProtect delivers a comprehensive and advanced level of protection, ranging from ₹1 crore to ₹10 crores, with an unlimited option available.

In this video, we have discussed ICICI Lombard's MaxProtect features, unique features , exclusions , waiting periods as well as calculated the premiums.


WATCH OUR VIDEO FOR MORE DETAILS :)

✅  To buy insurance policies, click here - https://hareepatti.com/contact-us/ 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
For any queries:
📲 WhatsApp : https://wa.me/message/VJCYEKX3EWHOE1
📞 Call : 9650706699
💌  Mail us at: info@hareepatti.com
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
✅ TIME STAMPS :-
0:00  Introduction
0:55  Claim Settlement Ration & Incurred Claim Ratio
1:13  Unique Features of ICICI Lombard MaxProtect 
2:01  Eligibility Criteria of  ICICI Lombard MaxProtect 
2:56  Features of  ICICI Lombard MaxProtect 
3:56  Variants of  ICICI Lombard MaxProtect 
5:39  Waiting Periods
6:07  Zones in  ICICI Lombard MaxProtect 
6:43  Premiums of  ICICI Lombard MaxProtect 
7:10  Conclusion

comments.text:
Thanks a lot mam!! Much awaited video mam. I’m having a query as I went though the policy wordings. Here it’s stated that  “world wide cover is applicable only when the policy holder is under 65 years of age at the time of issuance and must be within India at the time of issuance” 

Since health insurance policies are renewed and issued every year does it mean world wide cover will not be there even for renewals after 65 years of age? 

I’m asking this because this policy will be issued only for people before 65 years in first place then why did they include the phrase “under 65 years” in world wide cover paragraph as the whole policy will not be issued after 65 years right mam. 

Hope you got my query and expecting answer as the customer care keep repeating useless and already mentioned things over email support. Thanks again mam!!
